note: Assured Downstream is an agent system. CLI commands are local tool adapters that agents call; they are not the system boundary.

control_plane.state_store: SQLite event/work/handoff ledger plus catalog, lifecycle state, evidence manifests, and release evaluations
control_plane.mutation_policy: observe-first by default; repository, GitHub, and release mutations require explicit gates; outbound maintainer contact is disabled
control_plane.github_account_boundary: all GitHub mutations must satisfy policies/github-account-boundary.json; authentication switching and cross-account delegation are forbidden

execution_plane.local_backend: custom FOSS Python and SQLite with leased at-least-once work delivery
execution_plane.model_policy: Codex is constrained and advisory for judgment-heavy work; deterministic tools and policy gates remain authoritative
execution_plane.distributed_backend_candidate: Dapr Workflow and pub/sub after multi-host or Kubernetes operation justifies sidecars and control-plane services

agents.name: Source Discovery Agent
agents.purpose: Find candidate upstream projects from awesome lists, package indexes, advisories, GitHub search, user nominations, and maintainer requests.

agents.name: Catalog Ingestion Agent
agents.purpose: Normalize discovered repositories, deduplicate candidates, enrich metadata, and maintain the catalog.

agents.name: Triage Agent
agents.purpose: Score candidates, explain selection, apply allow/suppress policy, and choose first-lane work.

agents.name: Governor Agent
agents.purpose: Enforce policies, block unsafe transitions, require evidence before claims, and route uncertainty to humans.

agents.name: Fork And Sync Agent
agents.purpose: Create or detect downstream forks, configure remotes, sync upstream, and preserve secure branches.

agents.name: Recon Agent
agents.purpose: Inspect checkouts without executing target code and infer language, workflow, release, artifact, and risk signals.

agents.name: Overlay Planner Agent
agents.purpose: Choose the smallest safe hardening overlay for a repository based on recon and target assurance level.

agents.name: Patch Agent
agents.purpose: Render approved overlays and repository-specific changes while keeping diffs small and reviewable.

agents.name: Publication Request Agent
agents.purpose: Convert an approved local patch into a canonical, digest-bound, expiring secure-branch publication request without mutating a remote.
human_gates: all remote branch creation or update

agents.name: Publication Authorization Agent
agents.purpose: Verify a protected-workflow Sigstore/in-toto authorization against pinned signer, source, runner, predicate, subject, scope, and expiry policy.

agents.name: Secure Branch Publisher Agent
agents.purpose: Consume a verified one-time authorization and publish the exact local secure commit with an exact refspec and expected remote commit guard.

agents.name: Tooling Curator Agent
agents.purpose: Maintain approved tools, resolve pins, refresh lockfiles, verify tooling provenance, and retire risky tools.

agents.name: Build Result Agent
agents.purpose: Coordinate external builders and snapshot their outputs without treating builder declarations as independent proof.

agents.name: Trace Agent
agents.purpose: Capture process, file, network, syscall, and security-event evidence during builds.

agents.name: Attestation Agent
agents.purpose: Produce SBOMs, SLSA provenance, in-toto statements, signatures, evidence manifests, and verification guides.

agents.name: Release Verifier Agent
agents.purpose: Cryptographically verify retained Sigstore bundles, bind certificate-backed facts to pinned policy, and retain upstream lineage only as a workflow-authored claim until separately verified.

agents.name: Builder Verifier Agent
agents.purpose: Verify retained reusable-workflow Sigstore bundles while keeping caller, signer, source, target, and builder identities distinct.

agents.name: Repro Agent
agents.purpose: Run independent rebuilds and compare artifacts, SBOMs, materials, provenance, and normalized behavior.

agents.name: Release Agent
agents.purpose: Publish hardened releases only after gates pass and attach evidence for downstream users.

agents.name: Fork Publication Agent
agents.purpose: Publish upstream lineage, overlay summaries, evidence links, and optional fetch instructions inside the downstream fork without outbound contact.

agents.name: Watch Agent
agents.purpose: Monitor upstream commits, releases, advisories, tooling changes, policy changes, and downstream drift.

handoff_invariants: Every output event must reference a run id and source repository when applicable.
Every agent must write machine-readable artifacts before human-readable summaries.
Every mutation-capable agent must support dry-run planning.
Every release claim must be backed by evidence and a Governor Agent decision.
Fork publication must never create pull requests, issues, comments, email, or other outbound maintainer contact.
GitHub mutations must verify the configured actor immediately before execution and must fail closed instead of switching authentication or delegating approval to another user account.
